file_name: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated - A_R REMARKETING  AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Trust (together, the "Issuers") hereby appoint Barclays as exclusive remarketing agent (the "Remarketing Agent"), and Barclays hereby accepts appointment as Remarketing Agent, for the purpose of (i) remarketing the Remarketing Securities on behalf of the holders thereof and (ii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Trust (together, the "Issuers") hereby appoint Barclays as exclusive remarketing agent (the "Remarketing Agent"), and Barclays hereby accepts appointment as Primary Agent, for the purpose of (i) remarketing the Remarketing Securities on behalf of the holders thereof and (ii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "Remarketing Agent" to "Primary Agent" creates confusion as the document consistently refers to duties assigned to the "Remarketing Agent" in subsequent sections. This perturbation could lead to a dispute regarding whether Barclays' responsibilities are as a Remarketing Agent or Primary Agent. This pertubation has an in-text contradiction in Section 1(b) and Section 4, where the terminology of "Remarketing Agent" is consistently used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Remarketing Agent agrees to: (i) use its commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the Remarketing Securities deemed tendered to the Remarketing Agent in the Remarketing pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures; (ii) notify the Issuers promptly of the Reset Rate; and (iii) carry out such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with the provisions of the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Remarketing Agent" to "Primary Agent" directly contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to the duties of the "Remarketing Agent". This introduces a clear inconsistency and ambiguity, as the Issuers and Barclays may dispute their roles and responsibilities, which creates uncertainty in enforcing Section 1(b) and Section 4 and other sections of the contract. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, states that this can lead to conflicting obligations. This is applicable here.

perturbation.original_text: If there has been a successful Remarketing, the Company shall pay to the Remarketing Agent for the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder on the Remarketing Settlement Date, by wire transfer to an account designated by the Remarketing Agent, a fee in an amount equal to 25 basis points (0.25%) of the Accreted Value of the Remarketed Securities.
perturbation.changed_text: If there has been a successful Remarketing, the Company shall pay to the Remarketing entity for the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder on the Remarketing Settlement Date, by wire transfer to an account designated by the Remarketing Agent, a fee in an amount equal to 25 basis points (0.25%) of the Accreted Value of the Remarketed Securities.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "Remarketing Agent" with "Remarketing entity," it introduces uncertainty about who is supposed to receive the fee, despite the contract defining 'Remarketing Agent' earlier. This creates an internal contradiction. This pertubation has an in-text contradiction, because 'Remarketing Agent' is used on Section 4(a), creating an ambiguity on who is getting paid by this terminology.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If there has been a successful Remarketing, the Company shall pay to the Remarketing entity for the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder on the Remarketing Settlement Date, by wire transfer to an account designated by the Remarketing Agent, a fee in an amount equal to 25 basis points (0.25%) of the Accreted Value of the Remarketed Securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Remarketing Agent" with "Remarketing entity" in the clause specifying who receives the fee. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This introduces ambiguity because the contract defines 'Remarketing Agent' elsewhere, creating uncertainty about who is supposed to be paid. This misalignment of terminology creates a direct conflict with the intended meaning of the contract, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Remarketing Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, except if such liability is judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.
perturbation.changed_text: The Lead Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, except if such liability is judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Remarketing Agent" is changed to "Lead Agent." This misaligned terminology creates an in-text contradiction. This means the same person, the remarketing Agent, now can be called the Lead Agent, but without clarifying this in the text. By using Lead Agent, where it should be Remarketing Agent, is a conflict of terminology, and with that in consideration, it creates a direct in-text contradiction on where it should be refered as "Remarketing Agent", to now calling it "Lead Agent".
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Lead Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, except if such liability is judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.
perturbation.justification: While the explanation highlights a potential for confusion due to the change from "Remarketing Agent" to "Lead Agent", the provided contradicted text is identical to the changed text. There is no actual contradiction *between* these two sections, as they use the same terminology. The issue is only a potential point of confusion regarding which agent is being referred to, but if both are in fact the same as stated in the Explanation, the actual legal obligations are not in conflict, and would at most require an clarification. The definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' requires the interchangeable use of terms *without* definition, leading to conflicting obligations. Here, the obligations in both Changed and Contradicted text are the same, so it doesn't lead to conflicting obligations. Thus, no direct in-text contradiction exists.
